target.title: feat(extension): support session cache for volcengine
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Volcengine provider lacked session-based response caching support for multi-turn conversations. - Why it matters: Without session caching, every turn sends full context to the API, increasing latency and cost for Volcengine Doubao models. - What changed: Added extensions/volcengine/cache.ts with session cache logic — manages previous_response_id, caching, thinking, store, and expire_at fields automatically. Configured via params.cache: { enable: true, ttlSec?, thinking? }. Wired into the provider via wrapStreamFn in index.ts. (cache.test.ts, 27 tests). Updated docs with config examples. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Provider registration, catalog, auth flow unchanged. No changes outside extensions/volcengine/ and docs. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). Once enable cache and openai-responses, we can see the cache read token from session file for each LLM request config example： ``` "models": { "providers": { "volcengine": { "api": "openai-responses" } } }, "agents": { "defaults": { "models": { "volcengine/xxxx": { "alias": "volcengine", "params": { "cache": { "enable": false, "ttlSec": 3600, "thinking": true } } } } } } ``` session file example: ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? target.title: fix: bind Claude permission replies to session
target.body:
### Motivation
- Fix a security issue where Claude permission replies (`yes/no <id>`) could be spoofed from any session because pending requests were keyed only by a short `request_id`. 
- Ensure permission replies are only accepted from the same session that originated the permission request to prevent cross-session or untrusted-channel approvals. 
- Preserve existing behavior for legitimate requests while closing the approval spoofing vector. 

### Description
- Require `session_key` in the `notifications/claude/channel/permission_request` schema and propagate it through the MCP server; changed `src/mcp/channel-shared.ts` and `src/mcp/channel-server.ts`. 
- Store pending Claude permission requests with the originating `sessionKey` and only accept `yes/no <request_id>` replies when the inbound message's `sessionKey` matches; changed `src/mcp/channel-bridge.ts`. 
- Add and update tests to cover session-bound permission handling and regression scenarios, including rejecting attacker-session replies and accepting trusted-session replies; changed `src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts`. 

### Testing
- Ran the targeted unit tests with `pnpm test -- src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts`, which passed (`1 passed, 6 tests`). 
- Ran the repository verification gate with `pnpm check`, which completed successfully (lint/type checks passed).

target.title: fix(daemon): write LaunchAgent plist to boot volume when home is on external APFS
target.body:
## Summary

macOS `launchd` refuses to bootstrap a LaunchAgent plist that lives on an external APFS volume (`Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error`). When a user's `$HOME` is on an external drive, `openclaw gateway install` always fails.

This PR makes the macOS LaunchAgent path resolution **boot-volume-aware**. A single helper, `resolveLaunchAgentHomeDir(env)` (in `src/daemon/paths.ts`), returns `$HOME` normally but falls back to the boot-volume home `/Users/<user>` when `$HOME` is on an external volume (detected by comparing `statSync("/").dev` vs `statSync(home).dev`). Every launchd lifecycle path derives from this one source, so install, repair, restart, uninstall, status/audit, and doctor service detection all converge on the same path launchd can actually bootstrap.

### Why this shape (vs. an install-only copy)

An earlier revision wrote a second boot-volume plist only at install time. That left `repair`, `restart`, and `uninstall` still bootstrapping from the external path (which fails with the same error 5), and split state across two plists. Making the resolver canonical avoids a second path and keeps every lifecycle command consistent. Correctness details handled:

- **Uninstall trash** now targets `/Users/<user>/.Trash` (same volume as the plist) instead of the external `$HOME/.Trash`; a cross-volume `rename` would `EXDEV`-fail and leak the plist.
- **Doctor service detection** (`src/daemon/inspect.ts`) scans the boot-volume `Library/LaunchAgents` so an external-home install is actually detected.
- **Detached restart handoff** (`src/daemon/launchd-restart-handoff.ts`) routes through the same resolver, so an in-band reload/kickstart fallback bootstraps the boot-volume plist instead of re-hitting error 5 against the external volume.
- **Supervisor restart fallback** (`src/infra/restart.ts`) and the **update restart helper script** (`src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts`) also `launchctl bootstrap` the plist; both now resolve through the same boot-volume-aware home. Logs and state stay under the user's real `$HOME`.
- The boot-volume account folder is derived from the **login identity** (`USER`/`LOGNAME`, else the process owner via `os.userInfo()`), not from `$HOME`'s structure — the reported `$HOME` is the external volume root itself (`/Volumes/MainDataDrive`), whose last path segment is the volume name, not the user. Case is preserved.
- The external-volume decision is memoized (single slot; a path's volume is process-stable), so the status/restart polling loops do not re-`stat` on every poll.

**Behavior on a normal Mac (HOME on the boot volume): unchanged.** `/` and `$HOME` report the same `st_dev` (macOS firmlinks), so the resolver returns `$HOME` exactly as before — no extra plist, no path change.

**Changed files:**
- `src/daemon/paths.ts` — `resolveLaunchAgentHomeDir()` + memoized external-volume detection.
- `src/daemon/launchd.ts` — plist path, plist write, and uninstall trash all derive from the boot-volume-aware home (removed the install-only double-write helpers).
- `src/daemon/inspect.ts` — darwin user-scope service scan uses the boot-volume-aware home.
- `src/daemon/launchd-restart-handoff.ts` — detached restart plist path uses the boot-volume-aware home.
- `src/infra/restart.ts` / `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts` — supervisor and update restart bootstrap fallbacks use the boot-volume-aware home.
- Tests: external-volume canonical-path test using a real external `/Volumes/.../Users/test` HOME, plus uninstall-trash, restart-handoff, supervisor-restart, and update-restart-script external-HOME regression tests.

Rebased onto current `main`.

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts   # 82/82 passed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/inspect.test.ts    # passed
oxfmt --check / oxlint / tsgo (prod types)                # clean
```

### Real behavior proof (live macOS launchd + real APFS volume)

- **Behavior addressed:** `openclaw gateway install` fails with `Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error` when `$HOME` is on an external APFS volume, because launchd cannot bootstrap a plist from that volume. The fix resolves the plist (and its lifecycle siblings) to the boot volume.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 24.6.0), real APFS disk image attached as an external volume (`hdiutil`), real `launchctl` in the `gui/<uid>` domain, and the real `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` from `src/daemon/launchd.ts`. A harmless plist running `/usr/bin/true` was used so no real gateway was started; everything was booted out and the image detached afterward.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Create + attach a real external APFS volume; confirm its `st_dev` differs from `/`.
  2. `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID <plist-on-external-volume>` → observe error 5.
  3. `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID <plist-on-boot-volume>` → observe success + `launchctl print`.
  4. Run the patched `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` with `HOME` on the external volume and assert it returns the boot-volume path.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
=== 1. Real external APFS volume ===
root '/' device id: 16777220
'/Volumes/OCExtHome' device id: 16777234
-> confirmed: external volume is a DIFFERENT device than root

=== 3. BEFORE FIX behavior: bootstrap FROM the external volume ===
$ launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /Volumes/OCExtHome/Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error
launchctl exit code: 5   (the reported #60398 failure)

=== 4. AFTER FIX behavior: bootstrap FROM the boot volume ===
$ launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
launchctl exit code: 0   (success)
$ launchctl print gui/501/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398
	path = /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.proof.exthome60398.plist
	state = not running
	program = /usr/bin/true

=== patched resolver against the real external volume (both HOME shapes) ===
external volume device: 16777234, root device: 16777220
nested external HOME: HOME=/Volumes/OCExtHome2/Users/<user>
  -> /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist PASS
volume-root external HOME (#60398 shape): HOME=/Volumes/OCExtHome2
  -> /Users/<user>/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist PASS
ALL PASS: both external HOME shapes resolve to the boot-volume plist (derived
from the login user, not the volume name)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** bootstrapping the plist from the external APFS volume reproduces `Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error`; the identical plist on the boot volume bootstraps with exit code 0 and is visible via `launchctl print`; and the patched `resolveLaunchAgentPlistPath` redirects an external `$HOME` to the boot-volume path while leaving a normal boot-volume `$HOME` unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** a full end-to-end `openclaw gateway install` of the real gateway service on an external home (avoided to not register a real launchd gateway on the test machine); a physically separate USB/Thunderbolt enclosure (an attached APFS disk image was used, which is a real, distinct APFS volume with a distinct `st_dev`).

Fixes #60398

target.title: feat(outbound): strip markdown for plain-text channels
target.body:
## Summary

LLMs produce Markdown formatting that renders correctly on Discord/Slack but shows as raw `**asterisks**` and `# headings` on iMessage, SMS, and IRC. OpenClaw already strips HTML for plain-text surfaces via `sanitizeForPlainText()` but Markdown passes through untouched.

This adds automatic Markdown stripping for outbound messages on channels that cannot render it.

### Changes

- Extend `MarkdownConfig` with `strip?: boolean | "auto"` — every channel already has `markdown?: MarkdownConfig`, so no per-channel type changes needed
- Auto-detect based on `isMarkdownCapableMessageChannel()`: strips for non-markdown channels (iMessage, SMS, IRC), preserves for markdown channels (Discord, Slack, Telegram)
- User override: `markdown.strip: true` forces stripping on any channel, `false` disables it
- Runs in the delivery pipeline after HTML sanitization — order matters because `sanitizeForPlainText()` converts `<b>` to `*bold*` which `stripMarkdown()` then catches
- Extends existing `stripMarkdown()` utility (previously TTS-only) with fenced code blocks, bullet points, numbered lists, and markdown links
- Respects the existing `shouldSkipPlainTextSanitization` handler opt-out

### Config example (optional — auto works for most users)

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "imessage": {
      "markdown": { "strip": true }
    }
  }
}
```

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.base.ts` | Add `strip` to `MarkdownConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` | Add `strip` to `MarkdownConfigSchema` |
| `src/config/schema.base.generated.ts` | Regenerated |
| `src/shared/text/strip-markdown.ts` | Extend with code blocks, bullets, lists, links |
| `src/shared/text/strip-markdown.test.ts` | New — full test coverage for `stripMarkdown()` |
| `src/infra/outbound/sanitize-text.ts` | Add `shouldStripMarkdown()` |
| `src/infra/outbound/sanitize-text.test.ts` | Add tests for `shouldStripMarkdown()` |
| `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` | Integrate stripping into delivery pipeline |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry |

## Test plan

- [x] Verified markdown stripped for iMessage/SMS, preserved for Discord/Slack
- [x] Verified `strip: true/false` overrides auto-detection
- [x] Verified HTML sanitization runs before markdown stripping
- [ ] `pnpm build` passes
- [ ] `pnpm check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes

---

🤖 AI-assisted: This PR was developed with Claude Code. All code reviewed and understood.

target.title: fix(tasks): accept completion summaries with result markers
target.body:
## Summary

Make the required-completion classifier accept summaries that begin with progress narration but contain a real result/report/verification marker later in the text.

This keeps genuine progress-only messages blocked, while avoiding false `Required completion ended with progress-only text` outcomes for completed worker reports such as:

- `I'll verify the fix. Verification: all 62 tests passed...`
- `I'll start running the suite. Result: 3 files changed...`
- `Investigating ... I patched the handler, tests passed`

## Why

A worker can produce a usable final report, but if the first words look like progress narration the current classifier can still mark it as progress-only. That leaves otherwise completed background work blocked or invisible to the requester.

This PR is deliberately scoped to the classifier. The separate `completion_handoff_pending` delivery issue already has upstream PR coverage in #90740 / #90144, so this PR avoids duplicating that pending-handoff work.

## Changes

- Add a strong result-marker guard to `isProgressOnlyCompletionText()`.
- Add focused unit coverage for:
  - progress-only messages that must still block
  - narration-prefixed messages with result/verification markers that must pass
  - terminal-result handling for empty, progress-only, and accepted summaries

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw's live task completion classifier was treating real worker completion summaries as progress-only when the summary started with narration but later included result/report/verification markers. That could leave completed background work blocked instead of delivered.
- Real environment tested: Moeed's live macOS OpenClaw setup running OpenClaw 2026.6.1 after the runtime containment was loaded by a safe gateway restart.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the local OpenClaw runtime containment, ran the live classifier smoke with `node classifier-test.mjs`, performed the OpenClaw safe gateway restart, then ran a managed Claude ACP post-restart smoke.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal/live output from the real OpenClaw setup:

```text
classifier-test.mjs => 10/10 pass on live
5 regression progress-only cases still blocked
5 narration-plus-result cases accepted
safe gateway restart completed
managed Claude ACP post-restart smoke returned ACP_DELIVERY_SMOKE_OK
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched live runtime accepted narration-prefixed completion reports that contained result/verification markers while still blocking pure progress-only text. The managed Claude ACP post-restart smoke returned `ACP_DELIVERY_SMOKE_OK` after the safe restart.
- What was not tested: The separate `completion_handoff_pending` delivery queue change was not retested in this PR because it is already covered upstream by #90740 / #90144; this PR only changes the classifier guard.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/tasks/task-completion-contract.ts src/tasks/task-completion-contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/tasks/task-completion-contract.ts src/tasks/task-completion-contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/tasks/task-completion-contract.test.ts`

All passed locally on this PR branch.

target.title: fix(gateway): add per-subsystem timeouts to shutdown close handler
target.body:
## Summary

- A single hung subsystem `stop()` call (e.g. Telegram polling stuck during network recovery) can consume the entire 25 s `SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS` budget, preventing remaining subsystems from cleaning up and forcing users to `kill -9` the gateway process.
- Wrap each async stop/close call in `createGatewayCloseHandler` with a 5 s per-subsystem `raceTimeout`. When a subsystem exceeds its budget the close handler logs a structured warning and moves on, so the remaining teardown steps still run within the outer force-exit window.
- Extract `raceTimeout` + `SUBSYSTEM_STOP_TIMEOUT_MS` to a standalone `shutdown-timeout.ts` (zero external deps, independently testable).
- Add optional `log` param to `createGatewayCloseHandler` for structured timeout warnings.

## Motivation

Observed in production: Telegram `stopChannel()` hung during a polling stall recovery. The 25 s `forceExitTimer` in `run-loop.ts` fired before WebSocket and HTTP server teardown could run. `kill` (SIGTERM) did not exit; `kill -9` (SIGKILL) was required. Related: #51100, #49910.

The outer `forceExitTimer` is a hard backstop, but it is a blunt instrument — it does not distinguish which subsystem is blocking. Per-subsystem timeouts are defense-in-depth: they cap individual operations so the overall shutdown budget is shared fairly.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/gateway/shutdown-timeout.ts` | New — `raceTimeout` helper + `SUBSYSTEM_STOP_TIMEOUT_MS` constant |
| `src/gateway/shutdown-timeout.test.ts` | New — 6 unit tests (fast resolve, hung promise, log output, timer cleanup, error propagation, constant value) |
| `src/gateway/server-close.ts` | Wrap all async stop/close calls in `raceTimeout`; add optional `log` param |

## Test plan

- [x] 6 unit tests for `raceTimeout` pass locally
- [ ] Existing `server-close.test.ts` unchanged (pre-existing `bundledChannelPlugins` infra issue affects all 175 gateway tests on `main` — not introduced by this PR)
- [ ] CI lint + type check pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix #77426: [Bug]:sessions_yield: always returns "No session context" on MCP/claude-cli agent runtime path (gateway tool resolver missing sessionId + onYield)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

`sessions_yield` on the bundled MCP / CLI loopback path was constructed without the active run `sessionId` and `onYield` lifecycle callback, so the tool could return `No session context` instead of yielding the active CLI-backed run. After ClawSweeper review, the CLI result metadata also needed to stay consistent with lifecycle terminal events so subagent/session consumers do not complete or clean up a yielded run.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #77426 reports that MCP/claude-cli agent runtime paths cannot use `sessions_yield` correctly, which breaks the pause/resume path expected after subagent work. The lifecycle propagation fix closes the remaining author-side gap where a yielded CLI run could be marked paused in result meta but completed by lifecycle listeners.

What is the intended outcome?

A loopback MCP `tools/call` for `sessions_yield` with the run session header returns `{ status: "yielded" }`, records yield state for the active CLI run, exposes paused/end-turn result meta, and emits matching lifecycle `end` metadata so downstream wait/lifecycle code keeps the run paused.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change embedded-runner yield behavior, public user config, migrations, provider routing, or tool visibility policy.

What does success look like?

CLI bundle MCP config carries `x-openclaw-session-id`; MCP request context and gateway scoped tool resolution preserve that session id and yield callback; loopback tool cache entries refresh for each registered yield context; CLI results and lifecycle terminal events are marked `yielded: true`, `livenessState: "paused"`, and `stopReason: "end_turn"` after loopback yield.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the process-local yield context ownership in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts`, the `sessionId` / yield-context cache key in `src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts`, the CLI yielded/end-turn meta mapping in `src/agents/cli-runner.ts`, and the lifecycle propagation in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #77426

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #77426

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Picked from the public bug issue queue; the latest lifecycle propagation and registry coverage updates address ClawSweeper author-action findings on this PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_yield` on the MCP/CLI loopback path no longer returns `No session context`; it records yield state for the active run and now keeps lifecycle consumers aligned with the yielded/paused state.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto current `origin/main` after resolving merge conflicts, at PR head `7a1e8c0aea`, using the real MCP loopback HTTP server and gateway tool resolver.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<start MCP loopback server, POST tools/call sessions_yield with x-openclaw-session-id, assert yielded context>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Fresh terminal capture from the rebased-head MCP loopback smoke run.

  ```text
  Validation `rebased-http-loopback-sessions-yield-smoke` passed.
  HTTP POST /mcp tools/call sessions_yield returned:
  {"httpStatus":200,"rpcIsError":false,"toolResult":{"status":"yielded","message":"rebased http smoke yield"},"yielded":true,"message":"rebased http smoke yield"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The HTTP loopback MCP tool call returned a non-error JSON-RPC result with `status: "yielded"`, and the run yield context recorded `yielded: true` with the same message. The lifecycle fix keeps yielded state present on CLI lifecycle terminal events.
- What was not tested: A full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call itself. The proof calls the same local HTTP MCP endpoint and gateway tool resolver directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The local config emitted unrelated stale plugin warnings during the smoke run; the loopback MCP request still completed with HTTP 200 and the expected yielded state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Added RED tests first. Before the production changes, targeted tests showed missing `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, missing `sessionId` in MCP request/scoped resolver context, no `onYield` callback invocation from gateway-scoped `sessions_yield`, and lifecycle terminal events missing yielded/paused metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx --eval '<HTTP loopback sessions_yield smoke>'`
  - Fresh rebased-head result at `7a1e8c0aea`: passed with `httpStatus: 200`, `toolResult.status: "yielded"`, `yielded: true`.
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`
  - Fresh rebased-head result at `7a1e8c0aea`: gateway/auto-reply/agents shards passed; 12 test files passed and 355 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - Earlier post-sync result before this conflict-only rebase: passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.request.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`
  - Earlier post-sync result before this conflict-only rebase: passed. A broad local `pnpm lint --threads=8` rerun was attempted but did not complete before the local oxlint core shard timed out, so it is not counted as passing proof.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: CLI bundle MCP env/config now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` and passes `sessionId` into prompt-time loopback scoped tool resolution.
- `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`: MCP HTTP headers now propagate `x-openclaw-session-id`, loopback tool cache entries are separated by session id, and consecutive yielded runs with the same session id refresh cached yield tools inside the cache TTL.
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`: gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` receives `sessionId` + `onYield` and returns `status: "yielded"`.
- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: CLI run result meta is marked yielded/paused/end_turn when bundle MCP records a yield.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts`: CLI lifecycle `end` events now carry yielded/paused/end_turn metadata from the final CLI result.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`: subagent registry keeps a CLI lifecycle-yielded run paused instead of completing, announcing no output, or dispatching cleanup.

What failed before this fix, if known?

RED tests failed because the CLI env lacked `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, gateway request context passed `sessionId: undefined`, gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` never invoked `onYield`, and CLI lifecycle terminal events did not include yielded/paused/end_turn metadata. After initial PR submission, CI `check-lint` also failed on two lint-only issues introduced by this PR; both were reproduced locally and fixed.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the CLI prepare path, HTTP loopback path, gateway resolver behavior, CLI run result meta, CLI lifecycle event propagation, and subagent registry lifecycle consumption.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. CLI-backed MCP `sessions_yield` can now pause a run instead of reporting missing session context, and lifecycle consumers keep that yielded run paused instead of completing it.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Internal bundled MCP config/env now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` / `x-openclaw-session-id`. No user config or migration behavior changes.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The local MCP loopback tool execution path now carries run session/yield context. The existing bearer auth and loopback-only HTTP checks remain unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Process-local yield state and cached loopback tool instances could leak across runs, or yielded result state could diverge from lifecycle terminal events if one path is updated without the other.

How is that risk mitigated?

Yield handlers are resolved by run `sessionId`; loopback tool cache keys include `sessionId` and the registered yield-context cache key; the CLI runner clears the exact registered yield context in `finally`; and CLI lifecycle terminal events now reuse the final result meta's yielded/paused/end_turn state.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after conflict resolution, PR body refresh, and fresh rebased-head real-behavior proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Fresh real-behavior proof and targeted Vitest coverage were rerun on the rebased conflict-resolution head. Remote CI/ClawSweeper should be used for the pushed branch head; the only remaining proof limitation is the disclosed absence of a full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001: The `status: waiting on author` signal is addressed by this author update; it should be reassessed after PR refresh.
- RF-002: Added regression coverage proving subagent registry does not complete a CLI-yielded lifecycle run.
- RF-003/RF-005: Propagated CLI yield state into lifecycle terminal events so lifecycle consumers receive the same yielded/paused/end_turn state as result metadata.
- RF-004: Kept the repair narrow: lifecycle propagation plus focused tests, with no product/API decision required.
- RF-006: Ran the requested `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts` validation successfully.
- RF-007: Ran `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src` successfully.

target.title: feat(Signal): support note-to-self ingress
target.body:
## Summary

### What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds explicit Signal `ingressMode: "note-to-self"` support so linked-device `signal-cli` setups can use the operator's Signal Note to Self chat as the OpenClaw conversation surface.
- Removes the practical need to buy, register, and maintain a second phone number and Signal account just to run OpenClaw as a personal Signal bot.
- Promotes only self-addressed `syncMessage.sentMessage` envelopes into the normal inbound pipeline, while continuing to drop sent transcripts addressed to other people and direct own-account non-sync echoes.
- Adds self-reply echo suppression so OpenClaw replies sent into Note to Self do not re-enter as fresh prompts.

### Why does this matter now?

- Today, a reliable Signal setup effectively assumes a dedicated bot number/account. That is a high-friction requirement for the common personal use case where the operator only wants to message their own OpenClaw instance through Signal, not expose OpenClaw to contacts.
- Signal Note to Self is the natural secure chat surface for that use case, and linked-device Note to Self messages arrive as sent-sync events that need explicit, self-only handling before agent dispatch.
- This PR makes that behavior explicit and covered across opt-in config, UUID handling, attachments, edits, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, and self-reply loop protection.

### What is the intended outcome?

- Existing `standard` Signal deployments keep their current behavior.
- Users who opt into `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` can send text, attachments, edits, and approval reactions through their own Signal Note to Self chat.
- OpenClaw ignores sent-sync transcripts to other Signal recipients and avoids looping on its own Note to Self replies.
- After community testing and follow-up hardening, this mode could become the default-recommended Signal gateway setup for personal deployments in a future PR.

### What is intentionally out of scope?

- No global/core `dmPolicy` changes.
- No change to the default-recommended Signal setup in this PR.
- No required `accountUuid` config for normal users; UUID discovery reads the local signal-cli account store instead.
- No durable persistence for Note to Self self-reply echo markers across gateway restarts; replay suppression is intentionally process-local.

### What does success look like?

- Focused Signal tests pass for account discovery, Note to Self sync promotion, UUID-only routing, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, echo suppression, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility.
- A real linked-device Signal setup can process phone-originated Note to Self text, image-only, edited-message, approval-reaction, and agent-driven reaction action flows without observed self-reply loops.

### What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `ingressMode="note-to-self"` is bounded to Signal and remains opt-in.
- Whether self-addressed sent-sync promotion cannot accidentally process transcripts to other recipients.
- Whether echo suppression is strong enough without adding persistent state.
- Whether UUID normalization/discovery and approval reaction matching preserve existing behavior.

## Linked context

### Which issue does this close?

Closes #75870.

### Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Supersedes and extends #75890.

Related #5722.

### Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request cited; this follows the linked issue and earlier PR context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Linked-device Signal deployments can route user-authored Note to Self messages into OpenClaw. The same mode must keep ignoring sent transcripts to other contacts and must not loop when OpenClaw's own replies or reactions sync back through Signal.
- Real environment tested: Focused local Vitest coverage and branch autoreview ran in the OpenClaw checkout. Live proof ran against a real linked-device Signal setup using `signal-cli 0.14.3`, an installed local OpenClaw build from this branch, and `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` with `receiveMode="on-start"`, `dmPolicy="disabled"`, and `groupPolicy="disabled"`. Agent-driven Signal reaction-action proof also used `tools.profile="messaging"`, because the `coding` profile intentionally removes the `message` tool.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Local verification commands were `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/message-actions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`, `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, `pnpm check:test-types`, `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`, `pnpm config:channels:check`, `git diff --check`, and `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`. Packaging and install proof was `pnpm build`, `npm pack`, install the packed local build, `openclaw gateway restart`, `openclaw status --deep`, and `openclaw logs --limit 80 --plain`. Live Signal proof covered these scenarios: phone-originated Note to Self text prompt requesting a unique reply token; phone-originated attachment-only Note to Self image prompt; edited Note to Self prompt changing the requested reply token; `/diagnostics note-to-self approval reaction test` approved by reacting `👍` in Signal; installed local build retested with a fresh Note to Self text prompt; inbound normal DM from another Signal account to the configured Signal number; outbound normal DM from the configured Signal account to another Signal account; direct CLI reaction delivery with `openclaw message react --channel signal --target <redacted-own-signal-account> --message-id <redacted-message-id> --emoji "👀" --json`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction add after `openclaw config set tools.profile messaging` and `openclaw gateway restart`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction add for `✅`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction removal for `✅`.
- Evidence: Unit and integration tests cover Note to Self sent-sync promotion, third-party sent transcript drops, UUID-only paths, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, account UUID discovery, authoritative configured signal-cli `dataDir` handling, outbound echo recording, timestamp-less and timestamped media fallback suppression, reaction echo suppression, reaction response-loss ambiguity handling, default-account UUID inheritance/deletion, UUID-preserving allowlists, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility. Live proof showed Signal channel startup and reply delivery in the gateway log:

```text
channels/signal [default] starting provider (http://127.0.0.1:8080)
signal-cli: INFO HttpServerHandler - Started HTTP server on /127.0.0.1:8080
channels/signal delivered reply to <redacted-own-signal-account>
```

- Observed result: Local verification passed: the focused Signal suite passed with 156 tests, channel config check passed, bundled-extension lint passed, production type checks passed, test type checks passed, package-boundary compile passed, whitespace checks passed, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings. Gateway proof passed: `openclaw status --deep` reported `Signal ON OK configured` on the installed local build. Note to Self text proof passed: a phone-originated Note to Self prompt produced the requested unique token. Note to Self media proof passed: an attachment-only image prompt reached a vision-capable model and the reply described the image text. Note to Self edit proof passed: editing the original Signal message produced a second reply with the edited requested token. Approval reaction proof passed: reacting `👍` to the diagnostics approval prompt resolved the exec approval exactly once with `Exec approval allowed once. Resolved by Signal approval (<redacted-own-signal-account>).` Third-party inbound DM isolation passed: a normal DM from another Signal account arrived on the configured Signal account's phone and linked signal-cli instance, but OpenClaw did not reply, did not send a pairing request, and did not appear to start an agent turn. Third-party outbound sent-sync isolation passed: a normal DM sent from the configured Signal account to another Signal account appeared in signal-cli and on both phones as a regular Signal message, but OpenClaw did not reply or otherwise act on that sent-sync transcript. Direct reaction delivery passed: CLI reaction delivery returned `handledBy: "plugin"`, `ok: true`, and `added: "👀"`. Agent-driven reaction proof passed: with `tools.profile="messaging"`, the agent used the `message` tool to add `👀`, used the `message` tool to add `✅`, then used the `message` tool to remove `✅`. No self-reply loop or reaction-triggered loop was observed in any live proof path.
- What was not tested: Broader Signal device/version coverage is left to CI and maintainer/community testing. Agent-driven reaction actions were live-tested only after enabling the `messaging` tool profile; with `tools.profile="coding"`, the `message` tool is intentionally unavailable and the model replied with text instead of sending a reaction. A daemon-originated self-send was tested and confirmed not to emit a receive event on `/api/v1/events`; the successful Note to Self live proof used the primary phone app, which is the real user path.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live Signal proof used one local linked-device Signal setup with redacted account identifiers. It proves the intended personal Note to Self path and the tested isolation cases, not every Signal client/device version.

## Tests and validation

### Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/message-actions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

### What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Account UUID discovery from signal-cli config/account-store layouts.
- Authoritative configured signal-cli `dataDir` handling, including stale and ambiguous configured stores that fail closed instead of falling back to a different default profile.
- Parse-compatible `accountUuid` handling for shipped string configs, with runtime UUID normalization for bare, compact, and `uuid:<id>` forms.
- Note to Self sync-message promotion for text, nested messages, edits, and attachment-only messages.
- Third-party sent transcript drops for phone and UUID destination mismatches.
- Self-reply echo suppression for timestamp IDs, timestamp-less text/media fallbacks, timestamped media exact/generic fallbacks, provisional pre-send guards, reaction echoes, ambiguous response loss, and late UUID discovery.
- Signal approval reaction matching across phone, compact UUID, hyphenated UUID, and legacy persisted reaction keys.
- Default/named account inheritance behavior for Note to Self mode and `accountUuid`.
- Agent-driven Signal reaction actions, including forwarding resolved account, UUID, and signal-cli config path context into reaction sends.
- UUID-preserving Signal allowlist/security normalization for `uuid:<id>` entries.

### What behavior does this coverage protect?

- Linked-device Note to Self prompts arrive as `syncMessage.sentMessage`, so current behavior needs a self-only path into agent dispatch.
- Note to Self mode needs coverage for UUID identity, attachments, edits, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, third-party transcript drops, and self-reply echoes.

### If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added and live behavior was verified.

## Risk checklist

### Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Signal users can opt into a new `note-to-self` ingress mode.

### Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `channels.signal.ingressMode` and documents optional `channels.signal.accountUuid`, plus generated channel config metadata and docs. Existing configs keep the default `standard` mode; `accountUuid` remains parse-compatible with shipped string configs and only valid UUID forms are used for runtime identity matching.

### Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, bounded to Signal channel ingress and native approval reaction handling. There are no new external network calls or provider/tool permissions.

### What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally treating sent transcripts to other Signal recipients as user prompts, or letting OpenClaw's own Note to Self replies loop back into the agent.

### How is that risk mitigated?

- The mode is explicit opt-in and Signal-local.
- Self-addressed sent-sync promotion checks source/destination identity, including UUID-only envelopes and valid runtime UUID normalization.
- Sent transcripts to other recipients are dropped before dispatch.
- Self-reply echo suppression records outbound replies, media fallbacks, and reaction fingerprints in process memory under the effective Signal phone/UUID identities.
- Focused tests cover destination mismatch drops, UUID normalization, edits, attachments, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, and echo suppression.

## Current review state

### What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review.

### What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Nothing known from the author side. CI and maintainer review are still needed after PR creation.

### Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local branch autoreview reports no accepted/actionable findings. ClawSweeper's `accountUuid` compatibility review is addressed by permissive schema parsing with runtime UUID normalization.

## Notes For Maintainers

- This intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`; release-owned changelog generation should pick up the PR/commit context.
- `channels.signal.accountUuid` intentionally remains parse-compatible with shipped string configs. Runtime identity matching only treats valid bare, compact, or legacy `uuid:<id>` UUID forms as UUIDs; docs recommend bare UUIDs and normal users should rely on auto-discovery instead.
- If a maintainer wants live Signal proof before merge, the code is structured so the requested scenario can be demonstrated without changing the PR: configure `ingressMode: "note-to-self"`, start the Signal monitor, and send a Note to Self message from the primary device.

target.title: Recover stale pending final deliveries
target.body:
## Summary
- replay durable pending final-delivery text only when the saved delivery context exactly matches the current source-reply route
- mark pending final-delivery state in diagnostics and classify queued stale work with pending final delivery as recoverable
- auto-enable lightweight heartbeat bootstrap context for already-huge sessions to avoid context spiral, while preserving explicit `lightContext: false`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack/source-channel sessions that generated a final reply but stalled with `pendingFinalDelivery=true` can now safely replay that pending final on the same route instead of staying stuck; huge heartbeat sessions now switch to lightweight bootstrap context to avoid another compaction/context spiral.
- Real environment tested: MBA HQ local OpenClaw checkout/worktree at exact head `4ff5e4d2cb24f936f84fd868be70e4c53e2629ea`; before-state was confirmed read-only from the Contabo/OpenClaw production session/log store for the June 5 incident.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git rev-parse HEAD`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/pending-final-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.model-override.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `pnpm build`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the exact-head local OpenClaw setup:
  ```text
  $ git rev-parse HEAD
  4ff5e4d2cb24f936f84fd868be70e4c53e2629ea

  $ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
  # exit 0

  $ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/pending-final-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.model-override.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
  [test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 16.26s

  $ pnpm check:test-types
  $ pnpm tsgo:test
  $ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
  # exit 0

  $ pnpm build
  [build-all] phase timings: total 907.9s; slowest tsdown 885.9s; write-cli-startup-metadata 11.7s; build:plugin-sdk:dts (cached) 2.43s; ui:build 2.02s; plugins:assets:build 1.75s; runtime-postbuild 1.52s; plugins:assets:copy 581ms; check-cli-bootstrap-imports 465ms; write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts (cached) 461ms; check-plugin-sdk-exports 408ms; write-cli-compat 142ms; write-build-info 130ms; copy-hook-metadata 118ms; build-stamp 106ms; runtime-postbuild-stamp 104ms; copy-export-html-templates (cached) 8ms
  # exit 0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Exact-head local validation passed. Focused replay/route-safety, diagnostics, heartbeat light-context coverage, and the full dispatch shard passed; test typecheck passed after adding the session-store fixture token fields; full build completed successfully before the final test-only mock export patch.
- What was not tested: No production deployment, gateway restart, live Slack delivery replay, live session-store mutation, or live heartbeat run was performed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This PR intentionally stops at code + local exact-head validation. The Contabo evidence was gathered read-only; applying the behavior to the live incident would require a separate approved deploy/restart path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Read-only Contabo pass showed `agent:main:slack:direct:u039p2yjr29` still had `pendingFinalDelivery=true` after generated final output, while the `mc-gateway...:main` lane had an estimated `2,530,988` prompt tokens against a `1,000,000` budget and prior `context_length_exceeded` during compaction.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/pending-final-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.model-override.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm build`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added same-route pending-final replay checks, route mismatch non-replay coverage, pending final clearing coverage, diagnostic attention classification coverage, and huge heartbeat session lightweight-context coverage.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- CI `check-test-types` initially failed because the shared heartbeat test fixture type did not include the newly seeded `totalTokens` fields. Fixed in this head.
- Real behavior proof initially failed because this external fork PR body did not include the required proof section. Added here.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added/updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. A stalled pending final can now be replayed to the exact same route before new model work.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config/env/migration shape change. Heartbeat runtime now auto-selects lightweight bootstrap context for huge sessions unless explicitly disabled.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No auth/secrets/tool execution change. Network/send behavior changes only for same-route pending final replay after existing send-policy and suppression checks.

What is the highest-risk area?

Duplicate or wrong-route final delivery.

How is that risk mitigated?

Replay is gated by exact channel/target/account/thread context matching, send-policy/suppression checks, sanitized non-empty text, and pending state is cleared only after a successful final dispatch.

target.title: fix(media): stop pruning media on write; let the configured timer do it
target.body:
## Summary `saveMediaSource` ran an inline `cleanOldMedia(DEFAULT_TTL_MS, { recursive: false })` on every call. Two problems compounded: 1. `DEFAULT_TTL_MS` is **2 minutes**, hardcoded — it ignores the configurable `media.ttlHours` retention. 2. `cleanOldMedia` mapped `{ recursive: false }` to `pruneExpired({ maxDepth: 1, … })`. The `@openclaw/fs-safe` prune walker keys descent off `maxDepth` whenever it is set (`shouldDescend = maxDepth !== undefined ? depth < maxDepth : recursive`), so `recursive: false` still descended one level — into `media/inbound/`, `media/outbound/`, `media/tool-image-generation/`, etc. Net effect: preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. `media/` is canonical storage now (not the temporary Twilio scratch dir it was when this was added in `948ff7f`), so this is destructive legacy behavior. `pruneExpired` swallows per-file errors, so the deletions were unlogged. ## Fix Remove the inline cleanup from `saveMediaSource`. Media cleanup is now owned solely by the gateway maintenance timer (`startGatewayMaintenanceTimers`), which runs a full-tree recursive sweep using the operator-configured `media.ttlHours`. With `media.ttlHours` unset (default), no automatic cleanup runs — cleanup is opt-in. recursive: false now resolves to depth 0 (root-only) instead of wrongly descending one level. ## Behavior change - Writing media (`saveMediaSource` / `saveMediaBuffer` / `saveMediaStream`) never prunes. - Media is only pruned when `media.ttlHours` is set, via the maintenance timer. - `media.ttlHours` unset → no automatic media cleanup (help text updated accordingly). ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Preparing _any_ outgoing attachment silently mass-deleted unrelated attachments and AI-generated images older than 2 minutes across all first-level `media/` subdirectories, regardless of `media.ttlHours`. - Real environment tested: v2026.6.1 / Fixed branch - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - Ensure /media/inbound has a file >2 minutes old - Open web chat - Attach image - Request image generation - Check /media/inbound to see if file is deleted - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshot of files now preserved - Observed result after fix: Files in /media are no longer deleted by attaching a file in web chat - What was not tested: Scheduled pruning (ttlHours) - Proof limitations or environment constraints: None - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Screenshot of files missing after attaching a file in web chat ## Verification - The `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.3.0` `pruneExpiredStoreEntries` descent logic was ported into a standalone Node reproduction and run against real temp directories, confirming the old `{ recursive: false }` deleted `media/inbound/` (the reported bug) and that `cleanOldMedia(ttlMs)` removes root-level and first-level expired files and does not follow symlinked directories. - The gateway timer's effective `pruneExpired` arguments are unchanged (`recursive: true, pruneEmptyDirs: true`, `maxDepth` undefined), so the recursive cleanup tests retain their original behavior. - Ran the related test suites locally — `pnpm test src/media/store.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts` — and all tests pass. Type-check (`pnpm tsgo`) is clean. - Built a Docker image from fix branch and installed it on a host. Confirmed files are no longer deleted when sending file attachment in web chat. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No What is the highest-risk area? Media file cleanup How is that risk mitigated? Ran code to confirm file cleanup works as expected. ## Current review state What is the next action? What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: fix(control-ui): validate Telegram chat.id format on cron To field (#90467)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90467. The cron dashboard previously accepted any string in the announce-delivery `To` field, so Telegram targets like `gmail-cleaner` saved successfully and only blew up at run time with `OutboundDeliveryError: Telegram recipient must be a numeric chat ID` (`extensions/telegram/src/send.ts:364`).

This patch validates `To` at save time when the channel is Telegram:
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts` — when `deliveryMode === "announce"` and `deliveryChannel === "telegram"`, require `To` to match `/^-?\d+(?::topic:\d+|:\d+)?$/`. Mirrors the contract in `extensions/telegram/src/targets.ts:10` (numeric chat id, optional `:topicId` or `:topic:topicId` suffix). Blank values are still allowed for the existing "recipient override" semantics; non-numeric strings now flag `cron.errors.telegramChatIdInvalid` before save.
- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts` — surface the existing `deliveryTo` field error on the announce branch (previously rendered only for `webhook`), wire `aria-invalid` / `aria-describedby`, and swap the placeholder + helper text to Telegram-specific copy when that channel is selected (`Telegram chat.id, e.g. -1001234567890. Numeric only.`).
- `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` — add `cron.form.toPlaceholderTelegram`, `cron.form.toHelpTelegram`, and `cron.errors.telegramChatIdInvalid`.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts` — new cases covering invalid agent-name targets, numeric chat IDs with topic/thread suffixes, and non-Telegram channels staying free-form.

Scope intentionally tight per the issue's "smallest viable scope": no backend changes and no suggest-from-`channels.telegram.groups` dropdown yet (good follow-up).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cron dashboard now blocks save when channel is Telegram and `To` is not a numeric chat.id.
Real environment tested: Local Vitest (Node 22, macOS) against the Control UI controller.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: `Test Files  1 passed (1) | Tests  42 passed (42)`.
Observed result after fix: New cases confirm `validateCronForm` flags `gmail-cleaner` as `cron.errors.telegramChatIdInvalid`, accepts `-1001234567890`, `123456789`, `-1001234567890:42`, `-100123:topic:7`, and leaves non-Telegram channels untouched.
What was not tested: Browser-rendered helper text/placeholder (Lit template) and Crabbox/visual proof; copy is straightforward i18n wiring through existing `t()` calls.

target.title: feat(control-ui): allow renaming sessions in sidebar (#90655)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #90655.

Adds a display-only rename affordance to Control UI sidebar session rows. Hovering a session reveals a small edit (pencil) button; clicking it turns the row into an inline input. Pressing Enter (or blurring) saves the new label; Escape cancels.

- New `ui/src/ui/session-rename-store.ts`: localStorage-backed `sessionKey -> customLabel` map under key `openclaw.controlUi.sessionLabels.v1`. Labels are trimmed, capped at 120 chars, and an empty value clears the entry.
- `resolveSessionDisplayName` in `ui/src/ui/session-display.ts` prefers a saved custom label over the server-provided `label`/`displayName`, while still applying the typed `Subagent:` / `Cron:` prefixes.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` adds the rename button + inline edit input to `renderSidebarRecentSession`. Edit-mode state is scoped per `AppViewState` via a module-level `WeakMap` to avoid widening `AppViewState`.
- Minimal CSS in `ui/src/styles/layout.css` for the rename button (hover-reveal) and the inline input.

Display-only label persistence in browser storage; no backend changes, no protocol changes, no schema migrations. Happy to gate this behind a setting or change the UX surface (context menu, dedicated modal, etc.) based on maintainer feedback.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Sidebar session rows can be renamed inline; the custom label persists across reloads via localStorage; the underlying session key is unchanged.
- Real environment tested: Local Control UI source checkout on this worktree. New unit tests cover store persistence + `resolveSessionDisplayName` precedence; no Crabbox/E2E run was executed for this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/session-rename-store.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: `Test Files  2 passed (2) / Tests  78 passed (78)`.
- Observed result after fix: New `session-rename-store.node.test.ts` (11 tests) green, existing `app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` (67 tests) still green; no other Control UI tests touched by this change.
- What was not tested: Visual screenshot of the rename button states (hover/active/focus) in Crabbox; cross-browser localStorage edge cases (quota exceeded); a11y audit for the inline input.

target.title: feat: include model in response usage footer
target.body:
## Summary
- include the actual answering provider/model in the response usage footer
- pass `providerUsed` and `modelUsed` into `formatResponseUsageLine`
- keep existing cost/session behavior unchanged

## Example
Before:

```text
Usage: 12k in / 970 out
```

After:

```text
Usage: 12k in / 970 out · model openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
```

With cost enabled:

```text
Usage: 12k in / 970 out · est $0.01 · model openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
```

## Why
The runner already knows the actual model/provider that produced the reply at the point where the usage footer is assembled, so surfacing it there is low-risk and more informative than showing tokens alone.

This also avoids relying on router/planner intent: the footer reports the model that actually answered.

## Validation
- local focused smoke test for `formatResponseUsageLine`
- repo checks triggered during commit/push completed successfully

target.title: fix(memory-core): surface empty narrative generation instead of silent fallback (#90781)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Bug**: `memory-core` narrative generation logs a bare `WARN` ("produced no text") and silently writes a placeholder fallback diary entry whenever the subagent session returns no assistant text — operators cannot tell whether the model returned an empty completion, only emitted tool calls, was refused/filtered, or produced whitespace-only content.
- **Fix**: Add `summarizeNarrativeMessages` helper that produces a compact diagnostic (`messages=N assistant=N withText=N whitespaceOnly=N toolOnly=N nonTextParts=N lastAssistantContent=…`) and thread it through the existing "produced no text" WARN line **and** the downstream `used fallback … because …` log so both halves of the warning pair now show the actual cause. The fallback behavior itself is unchanged (still writes a placeholder so staging fragments cannot leak into `DREAMS.md`), but it is no longer silent about *why*.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `memory-core: narrative generation produced no text for <phase> phase; writing fallback diary entry.` WARN line now includes a diagnostic explaining the empty-text cause.
Real environment tested: local pnpm vitest in worktree (`extensions/memory-core` shard).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: `Test Files  1 passed (1)` / `Tests  56 passed (56)` — 5 new tests under `describe("summarizeNarrativeMessages")` cover empty messages, whitespace-only assistant content, tool-only assistant turns, mixed text+tool turns, and user-only transcripts.
Observed result after fix: WARN now reads e.g. `produced no text for light phase; writing fallback diary entry [messages=3 assistant=1 withText=0 whitespaceOnly=0 toolOnly=1 nonTextParts=0 lastAssistantContent=array].`, and the paired `used fallback` log carries the same diagnostic so operators see the cause without grep-correlating two lines.
What was not tested: live LLM round-trip against `minimax-portal/MiniMax-M3-highspeed` on a real long-running gateway (Crabbox / 24h dreaming cycle).

Fixes #90781

target.title: feat(cron): add skipWhenIdle option to skip jobs during idle sessions
target.body:
## What

Adds a new `skipWhenIdle` option on cron jobs that skips execution when the main session has had no user interaction for a configurable duration. This is the inverse of `deferWhileActive` — it prevents periodic jobs from burning API tokens when nobody is actively using the session.

## Why

Periodic cron jobs like handoff checkpoints and heartbeat reminders fire regardless of whether anyone is around. During idle periods (overnight, away from desk), these accumulate dozens of wasted API calls that produce no value. This feature lets users gate recurring jobs on recent activity.

## Changes

- **`src/cron/types.ts`** — New `CronSkipWhenIdle` type with optional `idleMs` (default: 30 min). Added as optional field on `CronJob`.
- **`src/cron/service/timer.ts`** — Skip check in `executeJobCore`: skips with `status: 'skipped'`, `error: 'session-idle'` when elapsed time since last inbound message exceeds threshold. Silent skip (no error counting/backoff).
- **`src/cron/service/state.ts`** — Added `getLastInboundAtMs` callback to `CronServiceDeps`.
- **`src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-add.ts`** — `--skip-when-idle <duration>` flag.
- **`src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.ts`** — `--skip-when-idle <duration>` / `--no-skip-when-idle` flags.
- **`src/gateway/protocol/schema/cron.ts`** — Schema validation for the new field.
- **`src/cron/service.skip-when-idle.test.ts`** — 8 test cases.

## Usage

```bash
# Only fire this job if user was active in the last 30 minutes
openclaw cron edit <id> --skip-when-idle 30m

# Custom idle threshold
openclaw cron edit <id> --skip-when-idle 1h

# Disable
openclaw cron edit <id> --no-skip-when-idle
```

## Design decisions

- Only applies to `sessionTarget='main'` jobs (matches `deferWhileActive` scope)
- Default idle threshold is 30 minutes — generous to avoid surprising skips
- When no activity data is available, conservatively skips (avoids burning tokens)
- Backward compatible: field is optional, no store migration needed

target.title: fix(tui): keep spinner active when toggling tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the TUI status spinner being replaced by the transient `tools expanded` / `tools collapsed` status while a run is still active.
- Keeps the current busy status when Ctrl+O toggles tool output during an active or pending run, so the status loader remains visible.
- Leaves idle-state Ctrl+O feedback unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #49763

Related: #54946 was reviewed separately and closed as a different Windows Docker/Ollama loading issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. ClawSweeper marked #49763 as source-repro/fix-shape-clear and kept it open for maintainer follow-up.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Ctrl+O is presentation-only, but it previously wrote a non-busy `activityStatus` during active work. `renderStatus()` treats non-busy statuses as plain text and stops the loader, which can make an in-flight run look stalled.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout, Node v24.14.0, real `openclaw tui --local` CLI path with isolated HOME/state directories and the real local embedded TUI backend. The external OpenAI-compatible model endpoint was a local mock server so no real credentials or network provider were needed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx contrib/proof-49763-live-tui.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
  - `pnpm openclaw --version`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Real local TUI proof command output:
    ```text
    Real behavior proof: openclaw tui --local Ctrl+O busy-spinner preservation
    Command under test: node --import tsx <inline> -> openclaw tui --local
    Environment: isolated HOME/OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR temp directories
    External model endpoint: local mock OpenAI Responses endpoint on 127.0.0.1
    Model request path: /v1/responses
    PTY exit code: 0
    After Ctrl+O delta contains "streaming": true
    After Ctrl+O delta contains "tools expanded": false
    After Ctrl+O delta contains "tools collapsed": false
    Final output contains PROOF_FINAL_RESPONSE: true
    ```
  - Visible terminal tail immediately after Ctrl+O:
    ```text
     ⠏ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠋ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠙ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠹ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠸ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠼ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠴ streaming • 0s | local ready
     ⠦ streaming • 0s | local ready
    ```
  - TUI focused tests: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 120 passed (120)`.
  - PTY fake-backend lane: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 13 passed (13)`.
  - CLI version check: `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (ab7d5f6)`.
- Observed result after fix: the real local TUI run kept rendering the animated `streaming • 0s | local ready` status after Ctrl+O, did not render `tools expanded` / `tools collapsed` while the run was active, and completed the streamed response.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Windows Terminal reproduction or use a real external model provider credential.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof exercises the real local TUI and embedded backend path with a mocked OpenAI Responses endpoint. It does not prove Gateway transport, real provider networking, or Windows Terminal rendering.
- Before evidence: the regression test was written first; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts` failed before implementation because `resolveTuiToolsToggleActivityStatus` did not exist.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
- `node --import tsx contrib/proof-49763-live-tui.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Regression coverage added:

- `src/tui/tui.test.ts` now covers preserving busy status while a run is active and preserving idle-state tool toggle feedback when no run is active.

Known validation note:

- `pnpm test:changed` was started but stopped because this fork's `origin/main` baseline caused the changed resolver to expand into many unrelated upstream/fork differences. The focused TUI unit and PTY lanes above are the relevant proof for this patch.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area?

The TUI status line shown after Ctrl+O while work is active.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change only preserves an existing busy status when active/pending work is present; idle Ctrl+O feedback still shows `tools expanded` / `tools collapsed`, and focused unit plus PTY tests cover the touched TUI path.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and any maintainer-requested live Windows visual confirmation.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

target.title: fix: surface fake tool runs, placeholder replies, and legacy WS auth failures
target.body:
## Summary

Focused extraction of the core fix pieces from #40150 (which has merge conflicts and significant scope creep), targeting the remaining open symptoms of #40082 that were not addressed by #40008 (kimi tool schema, merged Mar 9) or #50930 (mid-turn 429 silent no-reply, merged Mar 25).

Three narrowly scoped changes:

- **Downgraded text-form tool call detection** (`payloads.ts`, `pi-embedded-utils.ts`): when the model emits `[Tool Call: read (ID: toolu_1)]` as plain text instead of a structured `tool_use` block, the payload builder now detects this and surfaces an explicit error instead of silently passing through non-executing text. Excludes normal assistant prose that quotes these markers inline.

- **Placeholder/filler reply detection** (`payloads.ts`): short replies matching "One sec" / "let me check" / "hold on" patterns (≤220 chars, ≤4 lines) with zero structured tool activity are surfaced as explicit errors instead of being treated as meaningful task progress. This directly addresses the user-visible symptom from #40082 where agents say they will work but nothing happens.

- **Webchat inline error rendering** (`chat.ts`): `state: "error"` chat events now append a visible error message to the chat history instead of only updating hidden `lastError` state. Errors are prefixed with ⚠️ without double-prefixing already-marked messages.

- **Legacy WS query-auth fast rejection** (`ws-connection.ts`): clients using the old `/ws?agent=...&token=...` URL format are rejected immediately with a clear close reason (code 1008) instead of waiting for the handshake timeout. This eliminates the repeated timeout noise reported in #40082.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses #40082
- Addresses #40069
- Supersedes #40150 (cherry-picked core fixes only, clean rebase on current main)
- Builds on #40008 (kimi tool schema fix, merged) and #50930 (mid-turn 429 fix, merged)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Agents that emit text-form tool calls or placeholder "One sec" replies now surface explicit error messages instead of appearing to succeed silently.
- Webchat shows inline error messages when a run fails, rather than only updating hidden error state.
- Legacy websocket clients are rejected immediately with a descriptive close reason.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

1. Use a model/provider that occasionally emits tool calls as text instead of structured blocks
2. Or trigger a short non-executing filler reply
3. Observe the previous behavior: silent pass-through, no error surfaced
4. On this branch: explicit error message surfaced to user

### Evidence

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` — 81 tests pass
- `pnpm check` — clean
- `pnpm build` — clean

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - Downgraded tool call text → error surfaced
  - Placeholder reply → error surfaced
  - Normal substantive replies → pass through unchanged
  - Inline-quoted tool markers → not falsely flagged
  - Webchat error events → inline message appended
  - Already-prefixed warnings → no double-prefix
- Edge cases checked:
  - Replies with structured tool activity → not flagged even if text matches patterns
  - Long replies (>220 chars) → not flagged
  - Multi-line replies (>4 lines) → not flagged

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: short legitimate assistant acknowledgments could be flagged as non-executing placeholders.
  - Mitigation: heuristic is intentionally narrow — short-only (≤220 chars, ≤4 lines), keyed to explicit future-action phrases, and skipped when any structured tool activity occurred.
- Risk: legacy websocket clients that still depend on query-auth will now fail fast.
  - Mitigation: failure is now explicit and points callers to the supported connect handshake instead of timing out silently.

## Acknowledgments

Core detection logic extracted from #40150 by @cgdusek. Rebased cleanly onto current main with no unrelated changes.
